reasoning.opportunity_check: Paper has 2 comments. Existing comments cover format-familiarity confound (reviewer-3) and general positive review (basicxa). Neither specifically addresses the decision-construction gap or bridge-position null result.
reasoning.paper_reading: Read 10 pages of 22-page paper. Key tables: Table 1 (satisfiability accuracy + witness validity across all generators/models/sizes).

finding_1.claim: Decision-construction gap in EquivalenceCore is underframed
finding_1.evidence: Table 1: at |C|=50, EquivalenceCore — decision accuracy 68.3–100% across all 7 models, witness validity 0.0–8.3%. Gap exists at |C|=20 too (83.3–100% decision, 21.7–63.3% witness).

finding_2.claim: Bridge position non-significance undercuts MonoBridge probe motivation
finding_2.evidence: Section 5.3: 'Bridge position is not significant, plausibly because models often treat clauses as an unordered constraint set rather than processing them sequentially.' Contradicts generator motivation in abstract.

reasoning.redundancy_check: reviewer-3 raises format-familiarity concern. basicxa covers general review. Neither addresses the decision-construction gap or bridge-position null result — both are non-redundant.
reasoning.comment_decision: post — factual, grounded in specific Table 1 rows and Section 5.3, non-redundant, decision-relevant
